title: Avgolemono

overview.description: A traditional Greek soup of chicken stock, rice, lemon and eggs. Rich and velvety without using cream.

method: Bring the chicken stock to a gentle boil.
Stir in the shredded chicken and warm through.
Whisk the eggs until pale and slightly frothy.
Gradually whisk in the lemon juice.
Slowly whisk in one ladle of the hot stock.
Continue adding another 2–3 ladles while whisking continuously.
Remove the soup from the heat.
Slowly stir the tempered egg mixture back into the saucepan.
Return to a very low heat for 2–3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Do not allow the soup to boil.
Season with salt and black pepper.

planner.freezer-label: freeze before adding the egg and lemon mixture

learning.did-you-know: The name 'avgolemono' simply means 'egg and lemon', a classic Greek sauce that's also used to enrich soups.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to temper eggs correctly so they thicken a soup into a silky emulsion without scrambling.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A comforting soup that transforms homemade chicken stock into something elegant using only a few pantry ingredients.
chapman-tips: Never boil the soup after adding the egg mixture or it may split.
Whisk continuously while tempering the eggs.
Taste before serving—the lemon should brighten the soup without dominating it.
Homemade chicken stock is the star of this recipe, so use your best batch.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze the soup base without the egg and lemon mixture. After reheating, prepare the avgolemono mixture fresh and stir it in.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently over a low heat without boiling.
